one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG764-recommended digital edition of the Heart Manual — described by the AHA as 'perhaps the most extensively studied self-management book for patients recovering from acute MI,' with 500+ peer-reviewed citations since 1992.
why_it_matters_locally: The paper-based Heart Manual is the most extensively studied post-MI self-management programme globally, with foundational Lancet RCT evidence and AHA endorsement. The Digital Heart Manual brings this into a web-based format, maintaining facilitator-led delivery while expanding access. The paper version already serves approximately 14,000 patients/year in the UK.
target_problem_statement: Low CR uptake among post-MI and post-revascularisation patients, limited face-to-face CR capacity, and geographic barriers to centre-based programmes.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Conditionally recommended — evidence for non-digital Heart Manual; digital version evidence generation underway

evidence_summary: Extensive evidence for the paper-based Heart Manual: Lewin et al. Lancet 1992 (foundational RCT), BRUM RCT (Jolly et al. 2009), CHARMS RCT (Dalal et al. 2007), cost-effectiveness analysis (Taylor et al. 2006), systematic review (Clark et al. 2011), AHA/AACVPR/ACC Scientific Statement (2019). Digital version: pilot study (Deighan et al. 2017, n=28), patient-reported outcomes (Stark et al. 2021, Ranaldi et al. 2018). NICE notes evidence of clinical benefit for the non-digital programme; digital version evidence generation underway.

case_studies.title: Digital Heart Manual pilot study
case_studies.outcome: Results showed user-friendliness and effectiveness at communicating key messages.
case_studies.caveat: Small pilot study (n=28). Published in Patient Education and Counselling.
case_studies.source: Deighan et al. 2017, Patient Education and Counselling

live_sites: NHS Scotland (multiple boards), NHS Lothian (primary). Paper Heart Manual: approximately 14,000 patients/year in the UK and abroad.
patients_covered_note: Paper Heart Manual: approximately 14,000 patients/year. Digital Heart Manual-specific adoption data not publicly available.
dtac_note: Not within central DTAC records. DTAC approval is a condition of the NICE EVA recommendation.
device_class: Digital platform supporting cardiac rehabilitation — assessed under NICE EVA
cyber_notes: NHS Lothian public sector product. Operates within NHS Lothian clinical governance framework.
gdpr_note: NHS Lothian-operated product. Data governance within NHS Scotland framework.
target_patients: Adults recovering from acute MI, post-angioplasty, or post-revascularisation (angioplasty and coronary bypass without MI).
exclusions: Clinical assessment required. Not suitable as sole provision for patients requiring supervised exercise or those with contraindications to home-based CR.
onboarding_detail: Facilitators must complete specialist training from the Heart Manual Department. Available as a choice within the NHS CR menu alongside centre-based options. Web-based, accessible via digital links/QR codes.
supplier_wrap: Digital platform with post-MI and revascularisation editions. HCPs review and adjust goals collaboratively with the user.
local_wraparound_detail: Specialist training from the Heart Manual Department is mandatory for facilitators. Initial clinical assessment required. Facilitator-led delivery model.
training_note: Facilitators must complete specialist training from the Heart Manual Department. This is a prerequisite, not optional.
monitoring_model: HCPs review and adjust goals collaboratively with the user via standard NHS communication channels. No specific digital dashboard described.
monitoring_note: Facilitated programme. HCPs review progress via standard NHS communication channels. Not a continuously monitored service.
escalation_note: Standard NHS escalation routes. Facilitator manages clinical pathway decisions.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Standard NHS escalation routes apply.
implementation_prerequisites: Trained facilitators with Heart Manual Department specialist training
Initial clinical assessment by CR professional
indicative_price_text: Self-funding service within NHS Lothian. Only available via local NHS providers; cannot be purchased directly by patients.
service_wrap_note: NHS public sector product. Specialist facilitator training provided by Heart Manual Department.
contract_note: Information not available. Contact NHS Lothian Heart Manual Department.
expected_benefit_note: Taylor et al. (2006) demonstrated home-based CR via Heart Manual was cost-effective vs hospital-based CR. NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation.
tariff_considerations: Model using CR service delivery costs. Home-based delivery reduces facility and staffing costs compared to centre-based CR.
roi_note: NICE EAG model found cost-saving vs conventional rehabilitation. Paper Heart Manual has established cost-effectiveness evidence.
minimum_conditions_for_success: Facilitators trained by Heart Manual Department, established CR referral pathway, initial clinical assessment capacity.
procurement_notes: NHS Lothian public sector product. Contact Heart Manual Department for availability outside Scotland.

technical_integrations.device_integration: No device integration described. Web-based platform accessible via links/QR codes.

context_of_use.therapeutic_purpose: Structured home-based CR: exercise guide, education, diet, medication and psychological support
context_of_use.hcp_involvement: Active — specialist-trained facilitators deliver the programme
context_of_use.nice_scope: NICE HTG764 — conditionally recommended during 3-year evidence generation period

demo_notes: Contact NHS Lothian Heart Manual Department for further information.
source_summary: NICE HTG764, Lewin et al. Lancet 1992, Jolly et al. 2009, Dalal et al. 2007, Taylor et al. 2006, Clark et al. 2011, Deighan et al. 2017, AHA/AACVPR/ACC 2019.
confidence_note: The paper-based Heart Manual has one of the strongest evidence bases in CR. The digital version has limited direct evidence — evidence generation is underway. DTAC approval must be obtained. Verify all details with NHS Lothian.
